name: Climate Migration Dynamics
description: A system dynamics model capturing the interplay between climate change, migration patterns, and their broader socio-economic impact.

nodes.label: Climate Change
nodes.desc: Refers to long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns, primarily due to human activities.
nodes.example: Increased frequency of severe droughts in sub-Saharan Africa over the past decade.

nodes.label: Extreme Weather Events
nodes.desc: Increased frequency and intensity of events like floods, hurricanes, and droughts caused by climate change.
nodes.example: Hurricane Dorian's devastation in the Bahamas in 2019.

nodes.label: Livelihood Disruption
nodes.desc: Loss of income and resources due to environmental changes impacting agriculture, fishing, and other climate-sensitive activities.
nodes.example: Drought in East Africa leading to crop failures and loss of livestock, affecting millions' livelihoods.

nodes.label: Migration Rate
nodes.desc: The rate at which people relocate due to environmental pressures, seeking better living conditions.
nodes.example: In 2019, over 4 million people were displaced in countries like India and the Philippines due to natural disasters.

nodes.label: Receiving Area Stress
nodes.desc: Pressure on infrastructure and services in areas receiving a high number of migrants.
nodes.example: Increased pressure on public services and housing in cities like Dhaka due to rural influx.

nodes.label: Resource Scarcity
nodes.desc: A shortage of critical resources such as water, food, and housing in both source and receiving areas.
nodes.example: Severe water scarcity in Cape Town during the 2018 crisis.

nodes.label: Social Tension
nodes.desc: Rising tensions between migrants and host communities due to competition for limited resources.
nodes.example: Incidents of conflict between migrants and locals in refugee camps in Lebanon.

nodes.label: Policy Response
nodes.desc: Government measures to address climate impacts and manage migration effectively.
nodes.example: Implementation of adaptive infrastructure projects to counteract flooding in Jakarta.

nodes.label: Resilience Building
nodes.desc: Efforts to enhance community capacity to withstand and recover from climate impacts.
nodes.example: Development of drought-resistant crop varieties in parts of Ethiopia.

loops.name: Climate Displacement Loop
loops.desc: This reinforcing loop captures how increased climate change leads to more extreme weather events, disrupting livelihoods and escalating migration.

loops.name: Policy and Resilience Loop
loops.desc: This balancing loop illustrates how policy responses can enhance resilience and mitigate livelihood disruption.

archetypes.name: Tragedy of the Commons
archetypes.description: As migrants increase in receiving areas, the competition for limited resources creates social tension, worsening the overall conditions.

archetypes.name: Fixes that Fail
archetypes.description: Short-term policy responses might alleviate immediate pressures but fail if they do not address the root causes of climate change and migration.
